source_quote: Agriculture is the lifeline of Pakistan's economy accounting for 19.5 percent of the gross domestic product, employing 42.3 percent of the labour force
claim: PES frames agriculture as central to Pakistan's economy, citing its dominant shares of GDP and employment.
indicator_or_subject: agriculture GDP share and employment share

source_quote: CPEC will go a long way in the enhancement of agribusiness benefits by tapping value-added product innovation and supply chain
claim: The PES expects CPEC to benefit agriculture through supply chain development and value-added product innovation.

source_quote: performance of the agriculture sector remained up to the mark and achieved growth of 3.46 percent against the target of 3.5 percent and last year's meager growth of 0.27 percent
claim: Agriculture sector grew 3.46% in 2016-17, close to the 3.5% target and sharply above last year's 0.27% growth.

source_quote: This was possible by better harvesting of major crops through greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake
claim: The PES attributes improved agriculture growth to greater availability of inputs — water, credit, and fertilizers.
cause: greater availability of agriculture inputs like water, agriculture credit and intensive fertilizers offtake

source_quote: Cotton production was estimated at 10.671 million bales registering 7.6 percent increase over the production of 9.917 million bales during 2015-16 but was lower than the target of 14.1 million bales by a considerable margin
claim: Cotton production recovered 7.6% in 2016-17 but missed its target of 14.1 million bales by a wide margin.

source_quote: weaker than expected recovery in cotton production is mainly due to a sharp decline of 14.2 percent in the sowing area... due to exceptional losses from previous year's pest infestation and low domestic prices at the sowing time that pushed growers away from cotton
claim: Cotton recovery was weaker than expected because pest damage the prior year and low domestic prices reduced sowing area by 14.2%.
constraint_or_risk: pest infestation and low domestic prices at the sowing time
cause: exceptional losses from previous year's pest infestation and low domestic prices at the sowing time
effect: sharp decline of 14.2 percent in the sowing area

source_quote: production of sugarcane portrayed a very promising picture and reached to historical high of 73.6 million tonnes showing an increase of 12.4 percent over the production of 65.5 million tonnes during 2015-16
claim: Sugarcane reached a historical production high of 73.6 million tonnes in 2016-17, up 12.4% from the prior year.

source_quote: Rice area decreased due to decline in domestic prices of rice which reduced the area under the crop and growers shifted to sugarcane and maize crop
claim: Lower domestic rice prices caused growers to shift to sugarcane and maize, reducing rice sown area.
constraint_or_risk: decline in domestic prices of rice
cause: decline in domestic prices of rice
effect: growers shifted to sugarcane and maize crop

source_quote: The production increased due to better supply of inputs which contributed in enhancing per hectare yield
claim: Wheat production growth in 2016-17 was attributed to better input supply improving per-hectare yield.
cause: better supply of inputs

source_quote: Maize crop production stood at record high of 6.130 million tonnes during 2016-17, showing a major increase of 16.3 percent over the last year's production of 5.271 million tonnes
claim: Maize production reached a record 6.13 million tonnes in 2016-17, up 16.3% year on year, driven by intensive fertilizer use and area expansion.
cause: intensive use of critical agricultural inputs (fertilizers) coupled with increase in area sown

source_quote: local production contributed 0.462 million tonnes (14 percent) and the import share of edible oil/oilseeds was 3.264 million tonnes (86 percent)
claim: Pakistan depends on imports for 86% of its edible oil requirements, with local production meeting only 14%.
constraint_or_risk: heavy import dependence for edible oil — 86% of requirements met through imports

source_quote: substantial reduction in prices of all fertilizer products as a result of subsidy announcement by the government in June, 2016
claim: The government announced fertilizer subsidies in June 2016, including GST reductions and cash subsidies on urea and DAP, leading to substantial price reductions.

source_quote: Total offtake of fertilizer nutrients witnessed increase by 30.5 percent
claim: Fertilizer nutrient offtake increased 30.5% in 2016-17 as a direct result of the government's June 2016 subsidy and price reduction measures.
cause: subsidy announcement by the government in June, 2016
effect: Total offtake of fertilizer nutrients witnessed increase by 30.5 percent

source_quote: there is gap of 261 thousand tonnes of DAP which should be met through imports by private sector
claim: The PES projects a DAP supply gap of 261 thousand tonnes for Kharif 2017, to be covered through private sector imports.
constraint_or_risk: projected DAP supply gap for Kharif 2017

source_quote: The Gazette Notification of Seed (Business Regulation) Rules, 2016 took place on 28th September, 2016
claim: Seed (Business Regulation) Rules 2016 were gazetted in September 2016, formally enabling mandatory registration of seed businesses.
actor: Ministry of National Food Security & Research
actors: Ministry of National Food Security & Research

source_quote: The Plant Breeders' Rights Bill, 2016 was passed by the National Assembly on 5th September, 2016
claim: The Plant Breeders' Rights Act 2016 was passed by both houses of Parliament and signed by the President in December 2016, fulfilling WTO TRIPS obligations.

subsection: b) Importance of the Plant Breeders' Rights Act, 2016
source_quote: Many foreign companies are reluctant to invest in our seed sector due to lack of protection for their products because of the absence of an effective variety protection system
claim: Absence of plant variety protection laws has been a deterrent to foreign investment in Pakistan's seed sector.
constraint_or_risk: absence of effective variety protection system discouraging FDI in seed sector
cause: absence of an effective variety protection system
effect: foreign companies are reluctant to invest in our seed sector

subsection: b) Importance of the Plant Breeders' Rights Act, 2016
source_quote: We are hopeful that after the promulgation of PBR law our seed industry would experience a quantum jump in terms of investment and transfer of modern technologies
claim: The PES expects the PBR Act to unlock a quantum jump in seed sector investment and technology transfer.

subsection: e) Impact of Seed (Amendment) Act, 2015 Rules
source_quote: Private sector has been given larger role in all areas of seed testing, production, multiplication and processing
claim: The Seed Amendment Act 2015 expanded the private sector's role across seed testing, production, multiplication, and processing.

source_quote: a total number of 37,634 tractors were locally manufactured... witnessing a significant increase of 77.3 percent as the GST on locally manufactured/imported tractors have been reduced from 10 percent to 5 percent
claim: Tractor production surged 77.3% in 2016-17 (July-March) following the reduction in GST on tractors from 10% to 5%.
cause: GST on locally manufactured/imported tractors have been reduced from 10 percent to 5 percent

source_quote: An amount of Rs.31.716 billion was allocated for water sector's development projects/ programmes during the FY 2016-17
claim: The government allocated Rs.31.7 billion for water sector development in FY 2016-17, expecting 76% utilization by June 2017.

source_quote: Agriculture Credit Advisory Committee (ACAC) has set the indicative agricultural credit disbursement targets of Rs700 billion for FY 2016-17
claim: ACAC set an agricultural credit target of Rs700 billion for FY 2016-17, 17% higher than last year's actual disbursement of Rs598.3 billion.

source_quote: banks disbursed Rs.473.1 billion which is 67.6 percent of the overall annual target of Rs 700 billion and 22.7 percent higher than disbursement of Rs.385.5 billion made during the corresponding period last year
claim: Agricultural credit disbursement reached Rs473.1 billion in July-March 2016-17, up 22.7% on the same period last year despite real sector challenges.

subsection: Box-2: Credit Disbursement to Farm and Non-Farm Sector
source_quote: The increasing trend of non-farm sector is mainly due to more opportunities for Agricultural Value Chain Financing (AVCF) especially in Livestock/Dairy and Poultry sectors
claim: Growth in non-farm agricultural credit is attributed to expanding Agricultural Value Chain Financing opportunities in livestock, dairy, and poultry.
cause: more opportunities for Agricultural Value Chain Financing (AVCF) especially in Livestock/Dairy and Poultry sectors

subsection: SBP's Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
source_quote: SBP issued draft framework for Warehouse Receipt Financing... facilitates banks in development of specialized products for providing financing to farmers, traders, processors, and other players in the value chain
claim: SBP issued a Warehouse Receipt Financing framework to enable banks to offer specialized financing along the agricultural value chain.

subsection: SBP's Initiatives for the Promotion of Agriculture Financing
source_quote: CGSMF, with funding support of federal government... providing 50 percent credit risk coverage to Participating Financial Institutions (PFIs) on loans up to Rs 100,000 extended to small farmers
claim: SBP's Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers provides 50% risk coverage on loans up to Rs100,000 to enable collateral-poor small farmers to access credit.
policy_or_programme: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers
policy_or_programmes: Credit Guarantee Scheme for Small and Marginalized Farmers

source_quote: Livestock contributed approximately 58.3 percent to the agriculture value added and 11.4 percent to the overall GDP during 2016-17
claim: Livestock contributed 58.3% of agricultural value added and 11.4% of overall GDP in 2016-17, with value addition growing 3.4% over the prior year.

source_quote: Nearly 8 million families are involved in livestock raising deriving more than 35 percent income from livestock production activities
claim: Livestock supports the livelihoods of approximately 8 million rural families who derive more than 35% of their income from it.

source_quote: Government of UAE has lifted ban on import of poultry and poultry products from Pakistan. This would go a long way in promoting export of poultry and poultry products from the country
claim: Following an inspection visit by UAE officials, the UAE lifted its ban on Pakistan's poultry exports, opening a new export market.

source_quote: Royal Friesland Company acquired 51 percent of Engro Food Pakistan. This is one of the largest private sector Foreign Direct Investment in dairy sector of Pakistan amounting to investment of US $ 450 Million
claim: Royal Friesland Company acquired 51% of Engro Food for US$450 million — described as one of the largest FDI transactions in Pakistan's dairy sector.

source_quote: regulatory duties to the tune of 25 percent have been imposed on import of Skimmed Milk Powder (SMP) and Whey Powder (WP)
claim: The government imposed 25% regulatory duties on imports of Skimmed Milk Powder and Whey Powder to protect the domestic dairy sector and discourage synthetic milk.

source_quote: exports of fish and fish preparations have increased by 12.20 percent in quantity and 15.09 percent in value during 2016-17 (July-March)
claim: Fish and fish preparation exports grew 12.2% in quantity and 15.1% in value in July-March 2016-17, earning US$276 million.

source_quote: Human welfare and development is a basic right of every individual as enshrined in the constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan
claim: The PES Health chapter frames human welfare and health as a constitutional right, situating the discussion within a rights-based development discourse.

source_quote: SDGs Goal-3 and Goal-6 relates to health of people and under takes a commitment to end endemics of AIDS, TB, Malaria and other communicable disease by 2030
claim: Pakistan committed to SDG Goals 3 and 6, pledging to end AIDS, TB, and malaria epidemics by 2030.

source_quote: Pakistan has decided to translate SDG framework into National Development Planning. In order to operationalize SDGs, the federal government and four provincial governments have already committed to set up SDG Support Units
claim: The federal and four provincial governments established SDG Support Units to operationalize the SDG framework, with units set up in the Federal Planning Commission and Punjab.

source_quote: a National Health Program has been launched under the guidance of the Prime Minister by Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination (MoNHSRC). The program will help millions of poor to access quality health care services
claim: The PM National Health Program was launched under MoNHSRC to provide universal health coverage, offering secondary care of Rs.50,000 and priority disease coverage of Rs.250,000 per family per year.
actor: Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination
actors: Ministry of National Health Services, Regulations and Coordination

source_quote: in Pakistan only 2 polio cases has been reported this year till April 25th 2017 against 9 cases recorded during same period last year and the country is almost close to polio eradication
claim: Pakistan recorded only 2 polio cases by April 2017 down from 9 in the same period last year, indicating near-eradication.
cause: improved and routine immunization
effect: reduction in polio cases from 9 to 2

source_quote: the doctor population ratio stands at 1: 997, dentist 1: 10658 and hospital bed 1:1584. While national health infrastructure comprises of 1201 hospitals, BHUs 5518, RHCs 683
claim: Pakistan health infrastructure comprises 1,201 hospitals, 5,518 BHUs, and 683 RHCs, with a doctor-population ratio of 1:997 and one hospital bed per 1,584 persons.

source_quote: The country is spending 0.5 to 0.8 percent of its GDP on health over the last 10 years. These percentages are less than the WHO bench mark of at least 6 percent of GDP
claim: Pakistan health spending of 0.5-0.8% of GDP is far below the WHO benchmark of 6%, representing a structural financing gap undermining basic service delivery.
indicator_or_subject: health expenditure as percent of GDP
constraint_or_risk: chronically low health spending

source_quote: During 2015-16, total expenditure increased by 13 percent over 2014-15, and during current fiscal year (JulyMarch) 2016-17, the expenditure remain at 145.97 billion showing an increase of 9 percent
claim: Total health expenditure reached Rs.145.97 billion in July-March 2016-17 (up 9% year-on-year), following a 13% increase in 2015-16.

subsection: 1. Family Planning and Primary Health Care (FP&PHC)
source_quote: The FP & PHC program so far has recruited more than 100,000 Lady Health Workers (LHWs). LHWs services encompasses the health conditions of women and children through improved sanitation, birth spacing, iron supplementation, larger vaccination coverage
claim: The FP&PHC program recruited over 100,000 Lady Health Workers delivering primary health services including sanitation, birth spacing, and immunization to women and children.
policy_or_programme: Family Planning and Primary Health Care Program
policy_or_programmes: Family Planning and Primary Health Care Program

source_quote: During 2015-16, 8 million children of 0-11 months and 6.5 million pregnant women were immunized against 7 deadly
claim: The EPI program immunized 8 million children (0-11 months) and 6.5 million pregnant women against seven vaccine-preventable diseases in 2015-16, with pentavalent vaccine introduced through UNICEF.

source_quote: The program targets is to reduce the malaria burden by 60% in high and moderate endemic districts/agencies and eliminate malaria in low endemic districts by the year 2017-18
claim: The Malaria Control Program targets 60% reduction in burden in high and moderate endemic districts, and elimination in low endemic districts, by 2017-18.

source_quote: Pakistan has the sixth highest burden of tuberculosis worldwide and is among the high multidrug-resistant tuberculosis burden countries. The government has declared tuberculosis a national emergency
claim: Pakistan ranks sixth globally for tuberculosis burden and is a high MDR-TB country; the government declared TB a national emergency and is implementing the DOTS strategy.
constraint_or_risk: sixth highest TB burden globally; MDR-TB country

source_quote: Incidence of TB stands at 231 per 100,000 population
claim: TB incidence in Pakistan stands at 231 per 100,000 population.

subsection: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis in Pakistan
source_quote: The program also intends to decrease more than half of new cases of hepatitis B and C through
claim: The PM Hepatitis Program aims to halve new cases of hepatitis B and C through preventive interventions and subsidized treatment for patients who cannot afford the high cost of medicines.
indicator_or_subject: hepatitis B and C incidence reduction target
policy_or_programme: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis
policy_or_programmes: Prime Minister Program for Prevention and Control of Hepatitis

source_quote: Currently, there are eighteen cancer hospitals functioning in different cities. These hospitals are equipped with latest and advanced facilities in Diagnostic/ Therapeutic Nuclear Medicine
claim: Pakistan Atomic Energy Commission operates 18 cancer hospitals across different cities equipped with diagnostic and therapeutic nuclear medicine facilities.

source_quote: The achievements of the health sector during 2016-17 included establishment of 7 Rural Health Centers (RHCs) 32 Basic Health Units (BHUs) and up gradation of 10 existing RHCs and 37 BHUs. The manpower included the addition of 4500 new doctors, 450 dentists, 3500 Nurses
claim: Health sector 2016-17 achievements: 7 new RHCs, 32 new BHUs, upgradation of 10 RHCs and 37 BHUs, plus 4,500 new doctors, 450 dentists, and 3,500 nurses added.

source_quote: Almost half of the population is suffering from high rates of malnutrition particularly children and mothers
claim: Almost half of Pakistan population suffers from malnutrition, with children and mothers disproportionately affected.
constraint_or_risk: widespread malnutrition affecting nearly half the population

source_quote: The caloric availability through major food items has remained at about 2485 calories a day per person
claim: Per capita caloric availability stands at approximately 2,485 calories per day, with food availability improving in 2016-17 through adjusted imports and exports.

source_quote: Federal Government has allocated Rs. 100 million in PSDP for the National Initiative for SDGs/Nutrition for next three years
claim: Federal government allocated Rs.100 million in the PSDP for the National Initiative for SDGs/Nutrition, with a PC-1 under preparation to deploy funds toward nutrition-sensitive activities over three years.

source_quote: Health profile of Pakistan is characterized by high population growth. Despite a significant investment in health sector over the year, the rate of progress is lower than most other countries. The disease pattern is heavily dominated by malnutrition and poor dietary practices
claim: Pakistan health progress lags behind most other countries despite significant investment, with disease burden dominated by malnutrition and poor dietary practices compounded by high population growth.
constraint_or_risk: progress lower than most other countries; disease dominated by malnutrition
cause: high population growth, poor dietary practices

source_quote: World imports had been stagnant between 2011 and 2014 but registered significant drop since early 2015 because of weak commodity and product prices and weak global economic activity
claim: Global trade contracted sharply from early 2015 due to weak commodity prices and sluggish OECD growth, creating an unfavourable external environment for Pakistan exports.
constraint_or_risk: stagnant and then declining world imports since 2015
cause: weak commodity prices, weak global economic activity
effect: adverse external environment for Pakistan exports

source_quote: Pakistan's external sector continued to face severe stress during 2016-17. Though the rate of export decline was stemmed, Pakistan's exports declined by 3.06 % during the first 9 months of the fiscal year 2016-17
claim: Pakistan exports declined 3.06% in July-March 2016-17, an improvement over the 12.9% decline in the same period a year earlier, but the external sector continued to face severe stress.
constraint_or_risk: external sector under severe stress

source_quote: Imports, however, continued to grow at a much faster rate and grew by a large percentage of 18.67 during the first nine months of the FY2017
claim: Imports grew 18.67% in July-March FY2017, driven by CPEC-related energy and machinery imports, creating a large trade imbalance.
constraint_or_risk: rapidly rising import bill worsening trade deficit
cause: CPEC construction activity, energy sector imports

source_quote: Measures announced by Prime Minister in January 2017 to be implemented during eighteen months. The Package 'Export led growth' has started showing a positive impact
claim: The PM announced the Export-led Growth Package in January 2017 for implementation over 18 months; early signs of positive impact on export performance have emerged.
cause: PM export support package

source_quote: China-Pakistan Free Trade Agreement (CPFTA) on trade in goods was signed on 24th November, 2006 and implemented from 1st July 2007. FTA on Trade in Services was signed on 21st February 2009. The FTA covers more than 7000 tariff lines at the 8 digit level of HS code
claim: The China-Pakistan FTA covers over 7,000 tariff lines, encompassing both goods (since 2007) and services (since 2009), forming a key pillar of Pakistan-China trade expansion.

subsection: Impact of TLP on Pakistan Trade with SAARC Region
source_quote: In FY 2003-04, Pakistan's total export to the SAARC region was US$ 886 million which increased to US$ 1,564 million in FY 2006-07 (in the first year of SAFTA's implementation).During FY 2015-16, Pakistan's exports to the SAARC region were US$ 2690.23 million
claim: Pakistan exports to the SAARC region grew from US$886 million in 2003-04 to US$2,690.23 million in 2015-16, with SAFTA and the Trade Liberalization Programme contributing substantially to this growth.
cause: SAFTA trade liberalization

source_quote: Exports during July-March, 2016-17 decreased to US$ 15.119 billion from US$15.597 billion during July-March, 2015-16, registering a decrease of 3.06 percent compared to 12.9 percent witnessed during same period in 2015-16 over 2014-15
claim: Exports fell to US$15.119 billion in July-March 2016-17, a 3.06% decline, but significantly better than the 12.9% decline in the same period of 2015-16, indicating the export decline is bottoming out.

source_quote: Fish & fish preparation subgroup during JulyMarch FY2017 witnessed a growth of 15.1 percent in value and 12.2 percent in quantity, compared to last year
claim: Fish and fish preparation exports grew 15.1% in value and 12.2% in quantity in July-March FY2017 year-on-year, with a particularly strong March showing of 25.2% value growth.

source_quote: exports, witnessed a marginal decline of 0.9 percent during July - March, FY2017 compared to the corresponding period last year. This decline was 8.2 percent last year (2015-16 over 2014-15). This is very encouraging and suggests that government steps and initiatives to boost the textile exports have started bearing fruit
claim: Textile exports declined only marginally at 0.9% in July-March FY2017, compared to an 8.2% decline in the prior year period, with the PES attributing the improvement to government initiatives.
cause: government export promotion initiatives

source_quote: Pakistani exporter has lost the share in Turkey as a result of imposition of 42 percent additional custom duty on Pakistan's hand knitted carpet's import in Turkey
claim: Pakistan's carpet exports to Turkey have been severely hit by Turkey's imposition of 42% additional custom duty on hand-knitted carpets, leading to a 17.3% value decline in carpet exports in July-March FY2017.
constraint_or_risk: 42% additional custom duty on Pakistan carpets imposed by Turkey
cause: Turkey tariff imposition
effect: loss of Turkey market share for Pakistan carpet exports

source_quote: The composition of Pakistan's exports more or less remained same during 2016-17 compared to previous years showing high concentration in few items like cotton & cotton manufactures, leather, rice, and few more items. The first three categories of exports accounts for 71.8 percent of total exports during July-March FY2017
claim: Pakistan exports remain highly concentrated, with cotton and cotton manufactures, leather, and rice accounting for 71.8% of total exports in July-March FY2017, a structural rigidity unchanged from prior years.
constraint_or_risk: high export concentration in few commodities

subsection: Box-2: Evaluation of Pakistan exports to European Union (EU) under the GSP Plus regime
source_quote: Pakistan duty free access granted by EU under its GSP+ Scheme, Pakistan's exports to EU have increased by 38% in 2016 over 2013. Pakistan's exports to EU have increased from 4.52 billion Euros in 2013 to 6.28 billion in 2016
claim: Pakistan exports to the EU grew 38% from 4.52 billion Euros in 2013 to 6.28 billion Euros in 2016, driven by duty-free access under the EU GSP+ scheme.
indicator_or_subject: Pakistan exports to EU under GSP+
cause: EU GSP+ duty-free market access
effect: 38% growth in Pakistan exports to EU

subsection: 1. Impact of GSP Plus on exports of Textile products to EU
source_quote: Textiles sector has been a major beneficiary of EU's GSP+ Scheme. Pakistan's exports of Textiles have increased by 55% in value terms in 2016 over 2013. Pakistan's exports also registered an increase of 33% in terms of quantity during the same period
claim: Pakistan textile exports to the EU surged 55% in value and 33% in quantity from 2013 to 2016, making the textile sector the primary beneficiary of the EU GSP+ scheme.
indicator_or_subject: textile exports to EU under GSP+
cause: EU GSP+ duty-free access for textiles
effect: 55% value and 33% quantity growth in textile exports to EU

source_quote: During current fiscal year, Jul-Mar period, Pakistan's imports are showing rising trend at a relatively faster rate (18.7 percent) due to the increased economic activity as part of ChinaPakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC), particularly in the Energy sector. The construction projects under CPEC require heavy machinery that has to be imported
claim: CPEC-driven construction and energy activity is the primary driver of import surge in FY2017, with heavy machinery for CPEC projects directly contributing to the 18.7% import growth in July-March 2016-17.
constraint_or_risk: rising import bill widening trade deficit
cause: CPEC construction and energy projects requiring heavy machinery imports

source_quote: Pakistan imports from countries like China, Saud Arabia, UAE, and Indonesia constitutes around 50 percent of the total imports. During current fiscal year share of imports from China has increased from 27 percent in last fiscal year to 28 percent during Jul-March 2016-17
claim: China, Saudi Arabia, UAE, and Indonesia supply around 50% of Pakistan's total imports; China's share edged up from 27% to 28% in July-March 2016-17, reflecting CPEC-related procurement.
cause: CPEC-related procurement from China

source_quote: the trade deficit grew by 33.1 percent to US$ 17.8 billion in JulyMarch FY2017
claim: The trade deficit widened by 33.1% to US$17.8 billion in July-March FY2017, driven by surging CPEC-related capital equipment and fuel imports combined with declining exports.
constraint_or_risk: rapidly widening trade deficit
cause: surging capital equipment and fuel imports, declining exports
effect: 33.1% widening of trade deficit to US$17.8 billion

source_quote: The current account deficit reached to US$ 6.1 billion (2.0 percent of GDP) during July-March FY2017. This was mainly due to a 33.1 percent widening in the trade deficit, which amounted to US$ 17.8 billion
claim: The current account deficit reached US$6.1 billion (2.0% of GDP) in July-March FY2017, primarily driven by the 33.1% widening in the trade deficit to US$17.8 billion.
constraint_or_risk: rising current account deficit
cause: widening trade deficit
effect: current account deficit of US$6.1 billion

source_quote: The remittances reached to US$ 14.1 billion during July-March FY2017 as compared to US$14.387 billion and registered a negative growth of 2.3 percent as compared to last year
claim: Workers remittances slipped to US$14.1 billion in July-March FY2017, a 2.3% decline year-on-year, though month-on-month March showed a 20% recovery suggesting the trend may be reversing.
constraint_or_risk: marginal decline in remittances

source_quote: The capital and financial accounts recorded a surplus of US$ 5.1 billion in July-March FY2017, which was significantly higher than the surplus of US$ 3.6 billion recorded in July-March FY2016. Net FDI inflows rose 14.8 percent to US$ 1.6
claim: The capital and financial account surplus rose to US$5.1 billion in July-March FY2017 (from US$3.6 billion a year earlier), with FDI inflows rising 14.8% to US$1.6 billion.

source_quote: the country's total liquid FX reserves as on endMarch FY2017, declined to US$ 21.57 billion of which SBP US$16.47 billion and Commercial Banks US$ 5.10 billion. In October, FY2017 foreign currency reserves hit all time high at $ 24.03 billion
claim: Total FX reserves declined to US$21.57 billion by end-March FY2017 (SBP: US$16.47B, banks: US$5.10B), having peaked at a record US$24.03 billion in October FY2017 before pressure from a widening current account deficit.
constraint_or_risk: reserves declining from peak due to current account pressure
cause: widening current account deficit not fully offset by financial inflows
effect: FX reserves declining from October peak

source_quote: Recession in advanced economies, impediments at structural institutional and entrepreneurship both exogenous and endogenous factors are responsible of stagnant exports. However the resolve of the government to improve the exports and in this connection a number of initiatives have been taken. As a result, the negative effects of exports are bottoming out
claim: The PES attributes stagnant exports to both exogenous (global recession, commodity prices) and endogenous (structural, institutional, entrepreneurship) factors, but expresses optimism that government initiatives are helping the export decline bottom out.
constraint_or_risk: structural, institutional, and entrepreneurship impediments to export growth
cause: global recession and endogenous structural impediments
